description:
You are acting as the build-and-release “artifact manager” for a small development team.  
Instead of managing real UNIX accounts (no root access), the organization tracks *logical* users, groups, repositories and ACLs in a small file-based structure rooted at

    /home/user/artifactory

A brand-new container is delivered with **only** this empty directory present; you must populate everything that follows.

Objectives
----------
1. Create the directory layout shown below (any intermediate folders that do not yet exist must be created):
    /home/user/artifactory/
        ├── config/
        │     └── users.csv
        ├── logs/
        │     └── permission_changes_2023-01-15.log
        └── repos/
              └── lib-utils-binaries/
                     └── permissions.yml

2. Register two new logical users and their primary groups in **users.csv** (comma-separated values, *no* spaces after commas):

    username,group,created_at
    ci_robot,automation,2023-01-15
    qa_lead,quality,2023-01-15

3. Create the new binary repository folder **lib-utils-binaries** and store its ACL data in **permissions.yml** using the exact YAML structure and indentation below (two spaces for each nested level):

    users:
      ci_robot: write
      qa_lead: read

4. Record every change you make in **permission_changes_2023-01-15.log**.  
   The file must contain exactly five lines, each beginning with a timestamp enclosed in square brackets, followed by the event description.  
   Use the timestamps listed here verbatim so that automated tests can compare the file byte-for-byte:

    [2023-01-15 10:00:00] Added user 'ci_robot' to group 'automation'
    [2023-01-15 10:00:01] Added user 'qa_lead' to group 'quality'
    [2023-01-15 10:00:02] Created repository 'lib-utils-binaries'
    [2023-01-15 10:00:03] Granted 'ci_robot' write access on 'lib-utils-binaries'
    [2023-01-15 10:00:04] Granted 'qa_lead' read access on 'lib-utils-binaries'

5. Make sure all new files are world-readable but only user-writable  
   (recommended final permissions: 644 for regular files, 755 for directories).

Verification details
--------------------
The automated checker will succeed only if:

• All paths exist exactly as spelled above.  
• users.csv has *three* lines (header + two entries) with line endings “\n”.  
• permissions.yml matches the YAML block verbatim (including two spaces indentation).  
• permission_changes_2023-01-15.log contains exactly the five lines shown, with no trailing blank lines or extra whitespace.  
• File and directory permissions are at least as strict as requested (files not group-writable, directories executable).

Perform all actions in the terminal; do not hard-code absolute paths into file contents unless specified above; use only Bash-compatible tooling shipped in a minimal Linux environment (coreutils, echo, printf, etc.).
truth:
Expected final artefacts and their exact contents/permissions:

1. Directory tree:
   /home/user/artifactory                  (mode 755)
   /home/user/artifactory/config           (mode 755)
   /home/user/artifactory/logs             (mode 755)
   /home/user/artifactory/repos            (mode 755)
   /home/user/artifactory/repos/lib-utils-binaries (mode 755)

2. /home/user/artifactory/config/users.csv  (mode 644)
   Exact bytes (use LF line endings):
   username,group,created_at\n
   ci_robot,automation,2023-01-15\n
   qa_lead,quality,2023-01-15\n

3. /home/user/artifactory/repos/lib-utils-binaries/permissions.yml (mode 644)
   Exact bytes (LF line endings):
   users:\n
     ci_robot: write\n
     qa_lead: read\n

4. /home/user/artifactory/logs/permission_changes_2023-01-15.log (mode 644)
   Exact bytes (LF line endings):
   [2023-01-15 10:00:00] Added user 'ci_robot' to group 'automation'\n
   [2023-01-15 10:00:01] Added user 'qa_lead' to group 'quality'\n
   [2023-01-15 10:00:02] Created repository 'lib-utils-binaries'\n
   [2023-01-15 10:00:03] Granted 'ci_robot' write access on 'lib-utils-binaries'\n
   [2023-01-15 10:00:04] Granted 'qa_lead' read access on 'lib-utils-binaries'\n
